title: Patient-derived iPSC models suggest astrocytic involvement in DM1 neuropathology
author: M. Simoes, Anton
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1) is caused by a CTG-repeat expansion in the DMPK gene, producing toxic RNA that sequesters MBNL proteins and disrupts RNA  splicing. In addition to muscular symptoms, DM1 causes cognitive impairments,  suggesting central nervous system involvement. We model these effects using  patient-derived iPSC-derived neurons (iNeurons) and astrocytes (hAstrocytes).
steps_raw: Myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1) is caused by a CTG-repeat expansion in the DMPK gene, producing toxic RNA that sequesters MBNL proteins and disrupts RNA  splicing. In addition to muscular symptoms, DM1 causes cognitive impairments,  suggesting central nervous system involvement. We model these effects using  patient-derived iPSC-derived neurons (iNeurons) and astrocytes (hAstrocytes).
